title: Project Claim Admin
description:
Job Description

A Project Claim Administrator is responsible for managing the administrative aspects of project claims, ensuring that all claims are properly documented, submitted, and processed in a timely manner. Their job scope typically includes the following:


i. Claim Preparation & Submission

  • Prepare and compile claim documents, invoices, and supporting evidence.
  • Ensure all claims are accurately documented and comply with contract terms.
  • Submit claims to clients, main contractors, or relevant authorities.

ii. Verification & Documentation

  • Cross-check claim details against contracts, and work completion records.
  • Check and verify sub-con claim ensure the sub-con claim is claim accordingly to the job completion.
  • Maintain accurate and organized records of submitted claims, approvals, and pending payments.
  • Follow up on missing or incomplete claim documents.

iii. Follow-up & Payment Tracking

  • Monitor the status of submitted claims and follow up with clients on claim approval or SES status.
  • Communicate with stakeholders (e.g., project managers, finance teams, subcontractors) to resolve disputes or clarifications on claims.
  • Maintain a tracking system for outstanding payments and expected claim receipts.

iv. Coordination with Project & Finance Teams

  • Work closely with project managers to ensure work completion aligns with claimable amounts.
  • Liaise with finance and accounting departments for payment processing and reconciliation.
  • Provide updates on claim statuses.

v. Compliance & Reporting

  • Ensure claims comply with contract terms, regulatory requirements, and company policies.
  • Prepare reports on claims submitted, approved, pending, and rejected.
  • Identify discrepancies and recommend solutions to improve claim efficiency.

vi. Handling Disputes & Adjustments

  • Address claim disputes with clients or contractors and provide supporting documents for resolution.
  • Revise and resubmit claims, when necessary, based on feedback or clarifications.
  • Ensure proper documentation of rejected or disputed claims for future reference.

Requirement

  • At least 1 year of relevant experience with Diploma in Civil Engineering or related Engineering Discipline.
  • AutoCAD drafting knowledge is an advantage
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Good communication skills
  • Able to meet tight deadline
  • Able to work in a team as well as independently
  • Preferably Singaporean and Singapore Permanent Resident

Other References:

  • 5 Days Work Week, 8.30 AM to 6.00 PM.

postedCompany.description: Vistek Pte Ltd is a Singapore-based company providing one-stop network infrastructure solutions for the telecommunications and underground electrical utilities industry.
postedCompany.name: VISTEK PTE. LTD.
